disclaimer: Seed data compiled from public sources. Every row confirmed_by_counsel=false until ratified by immigration counsel. Not legal advice.

rules.notes: Form I-129 base filing fee for H-1B (and H-1B1) classification, PAPER filing. Online filing IS available for I-129 H-1B at a discounted $730 (G-1055 ed. 05/29/26, Appendix A: 'Paper Filing: $780 / Online Filing: $730 plus additional fees'). Applies per petition regardless of initial/extension/amendment/transfer. Small employers (25 or fewer FTEs) and nonprofits pay the reduced fee (see i129-h1b-base-fee-small-employer-nonprofit). Verified directly against the live G-1055 edition 05/29/26 on 2026-07-06.

Reduced I-129 H-1B base fee for small employers (firms/individuals with 25 or fewer full-time-equivalent employees) and nonprofit petitioners. Same $460 whether filed on paper or online (no additional online discount on the reduced fee). Verified against G-1055 edition 05/29/26 on 2026-07-06.

Form I-129 base filing fee for L-1A/L-1B classification. Small employers and nonprofits pay $695 (half, rounded). Verified directly against the live G-1055 edition 05/29/26 on 2026-07-06 ($1,385 standard / $695 small employer or nonprofit; no online filing option listed for L petitions).
Reduced I-129 L classification fee for small employers (25 or fewer FTEs) and nonprofits.
Form I-129 base filing fee for O classification. Small employers and nonprofits pay $530. Verified directly against the live G-1055 edition 05/29/26 on 2026-07-06.
Reduced I-129 O classification fee for small employers (25 or fewer FTEs) and nonprofits.

Form I-129 base filing fee for E and TN classifications, PAPER filing. Online filing is available for E-1/E-2/E-2C/TN at a discounted $965 (E-3 listed at $1,015 with no online option). Small employers and nonprofits pay $510 (paper or online). Verified directly against the live G-1055 edition 05/29/26 on 2026-07-06.
Reduced I-129 E/TN classification fee for small employers (25 or fewer FTEs) and nonprofits.

Asylum Program Fee paid by employers with each Form I-129, I-129CW, and I-140 filing (standard employers). Reduced to $300 for small employers (25 or fewer FTEs); $0 for nonprofits. Still in force as of 2026-07-06.

Asylum Program Fee for small employers, defined as firms or individuals with 25 or fewer full-time-equivalent employees. Applies to I-129, I-129CW, and I-140 filings. Per G-1055 edition 05/29/26, the I-140 $300 tier applies to a 'Small Employer or self-petitioner' (e.g., EB-1A/NIW self-petitions). Verified against the live G-1055 on 2026-07-06.
Nonprofit petitioners are exempt from the Asylum Program Fee on I-129, I-129CW, and I-140 filings.

Fraud Prevention and Detection Fee, $500, required for initial H-1B or L-1 petitions and change-of-employer petitions; not required for extensions with the same employer or for dependents. No small-employer or nonprofit discount. Unchanged as of 2026-07-06.

ACWIA education/training fee for H-1B petitioners with 25 or fewer full-time-equivalent employees. Applies to the initial petition and the first extension by the same employer; not to subsequent extensions. Exempt: institutions of higher education, related/affiliated nonprofits, nonprofit and governmental research organizations, and certain other filings (e.g., amended petitions without extension).
ACWIA fee for H-1B petitioners with 26 or more full-time-equivalent employees. Same applicability and exemptions as the $750 tier.

rules.source_citation: Pub. L. 114-113, Div. O, Title IV, sec. 402 (9-11 Response and Biometric Entry-Exit Fee)
rules.notes: Additional $4,000 fee on H-1B petitions filed by employers with 50 or more U.S. employees where more than 50 percent are in H-1B or L-1 status. Applies to initial and change-of-employer petitions (not same-employer extensions). Collection authority extended and currently scheduled to sunset September 30, 2027 per DHS/CBP spend-plan documents. A June 2024 DHS proposed rule (89 FR, June 6, 2024) would expand it to all extension-of-stay petitions; no final rule found as of 2026-07-06.

rules.source_citation: Pub. L. 114-113, Div. O, Title IV, sec. 402
rules.notes: Additional $4,500 fee on L-1A/L-1B petitions filed by employers with 50 or more U.S. employees where more than 50 percent are in H-1B or L-1 status. Same applicability rules and FY2027 sunset as the H-1B $4,000 fee.

SUPERSEDED. Original $10 H-1B cap electronic registration fee, first collected in the March 2020 (FY2021) registration period; last used for the FY2025 cap season (March 2024). Replaced by the $215 fee set in the April 2024 USCIS fee rule.

rules.source_citation: 89 FR 6194 (Jan. 31, 2024); USCIS FY 2027 H-1B Cap Registration Alert
rules.notes: H-1B cap electronic registration fee of $215 per beneficiary, set by the April 2024 fee rule and first collected in the FY2026 registration period (opened March 7, 2025). Confirmed unchanged for the FY2027 period (March 4-19, 2026), which also introduced a weighted, wage-level-based selection process. Paid via USCIS online account.

rules.source_citation: Presidential Proclamation, Restriction on Entry of Certain Nonimmigrant Workers (Sept. 19, 2025); USCIS H-1B FAQ guidance
rules.notes: One-time $100,000 payment (via pay.gov) required with new H-1B petitions filed on or after 2025-09-21 for beneficiaries outside the U.S. who do not hold a valid H-1B visa. Listed on G-1055 edition 05/29/26 as I-129 H-1B additional fee item 5: '$100,000, unless an exception has been granted by the Secretary of Homeland Security'; paid via pay.gov BEFORE filing, not with the petition. Does NOT apply to extensions, amendments, or changes of status granted inside the U.S., or to previously issued visas; national-interest exceptions available. Proclamation runs 12 months (through ~2026-09-21) unless renewed. LITIGATION as of 2026-07-06: D.D.C. upheld it 2025-12-23; D. Mass. (Sorokin, J.) vacated the implementing policy 2026-06-08 (fee held an unauthorized tax); government appealed to the First Circuit 2026-06-11; on 2026-06-12 the district court DENIED a stay pending appeal but GRANTED an administrative stay of its vacatur pending the First Circuit's ruling on the government's stay motion (filed 2026-06-18, still pending). The payment requirement therefore REMAINS IN EFFECT and USCIS retains collection authority as of 2026-07-06. Monitor the First Circuit closely - status can flip when it rules on the stay motion. Re-keyed from consular_pending__h1b_active (a transition key that does not exist in transitions.json) to the consular_pending status, matching the equivalent rows in rules_h1b_cap.json and rules_h1b_mobility.json.

Form I-140 PAPER filing fee. CORRECTION on verification 2026-07-06: online filing for I-140 now exists - the live G-1055 edition 05/29/26 lists 'Paper Filing: $715 / Online Filing: $665, plus additional fees, if applicable'. The previously dismissed $665 online figure is the OFFICIAL online fee. Employer must add the Asylum Program Fee tier ($600 regular / $300 small employer or self-petitioner / $0 nonprofit). No small-employer discount on the I-140 base fee itself.

Form I-485 PAPER filing fee for applicants 14 or older. CORRECTION on verification 2026-07-06: online filing for I-485 now exists - the live G-1055 edition 05/29/26 lists 'Paper Filing: $1,440 / Online Filing: $1,390'. Includes biometric services (no separate biometric fee since 2024-04-01). No bundled ancillary discount: concurrently filed I-765 costs a reduced $260 and I-131 costs $630 paper/$580 online (typical concurrent EB paper package: $1,440 + $260 + $630 = $2,330).

Reduced I-485 fee for a child under 14 filing concurrently with a parent's I-485, PAPER filing. Verified directly against the live G-1055 edition 05/29/26 on 2026-07-06: 'Paper Filing: $950 / Online Filing: $900'.

Current biometric policy: the former separate $85 biometric services fee was eliminated/incorporated into the underlying filing fee for nearly all benefit types effective 2024-04-01 (a separate $30 biometric fee remains only for TPS and certain EOIR filings). Applicants may still be scheduled for biometrics appointments; there is just no separate charge.
Form I-765 paper filing fee (includes F-1 OPT/STEM OPT categories (c)(3)(A), (c)(3)(B), (c)(3)(C) when filed on paper).

Form I-765 online filing fee ($50 discount vs paper). F-1 pre-completion OPT, post-completion OPT, and STEM OPT extension applicants ((c)(3)(A)/(B)/(C)) are eligible to file online via a USCIS online account.

rules.source_citation: USCIS Form I-765 page; USCIS online filing announcements
rules.notes: F-1 students applying for OPT ((c)(3)(A) pre-completion, (c)(3)(B) post-completion) and STEM OPT extension ((c)(3)(C)) may file I-765 online; online filing for these categories opened April 12, 2021. Online fee $470, paper $520. Premium processing (I-907) is also available for these categories.

Reduced (half) I-765 fee of $260 for applicants who filed Form I-485 with the full fee on or after 2024-04-01 and file I-765 concurrently or while the I-485 remains pending. Renewals while the I-485 is pending also qualify for $260.

rules.value: HR-1 (Pub. L. 119-21) statutory EAD fees apply ONLY to asylum-applicant, parolee, and TPS-based I-765 categories (initial fees effective 2025-07-22 at $550/$275). FY2026 inflation-adjusted amounts, effective for filings postmarked on or after 2026-01-01: $560 initial EAD (asylum (c)(8) special ABC, parolee (c)(11)/(c)(34), TPS (a)(12)/(c)(19)); $280 renewal/extension EAD for parole and TPS categories; asylum-applicant renewal stays $275 (Pub. L. 119-21 sec. 100011 provides no inflation adjustment). These fees do NOT apply to F-1 OPT/STEM OPT, H-4, L-2, or adjustment-based I-765 filings, which continue to use the regular G-1055 fees.
rules.notes: Included so the platform does not misapply HR-1 EAD surcharges to employment-based/F-1 categories. CORRECTION on verification 2026-07-06: the FY2026 inflation notice (2.70% CPI-U July 2024 to July 2025, rounded down to nearest $10) raised initial EAD fees $550 to $560 and parole/TPS renewal fees $275 to $280 effective 2026-01-01; the asylum renewal fee stays $275 because sec. 100011 has no inflation provision. Confirmed against both the Federal Register notice table and the live G-1055 ed. 05/29/26 ($560 initial; $280 TPS/parolee renewal; $275 asylum (c)(8) renewal). Related FY2026 HR-1 amounts: I-821 TPS fee $510, I-589 asylum filing fee $100 plus $102 annual pending-asylum fee, I-360 SIJ $250, I-131 re-parole EAD fee $280.

Form I-131 (travel document / advance parole) PAPER filing fee. CORRECTION on verification 2026-07-06: online filing is NOT the same price - the live G-1055 edition 05/29/26 lists advance parole with a pending I-485 at 'Paper Filing: $630 / Online Filing: $580' (the $580 online rate also applies to several other online-eligible I-131 categories; reentry permits are paper-only at $630). No reduced fee when filed concurrently with I-485.
Form I-539 (extend/change nonimmigrant status - used for F/M/J applicants and H-4/L-2/O-3 dependents) paper filing fee.

Form I-539 online filing fee ($50 discount vs paper). Note: online filing is limited to certain applicant groups; combined payments with I-129 may cause rejection of both forms per USCIS I-539 instructions.
USCIS exempted the former $85 biometric services fee for all I-539 applicants effective October 1, 2023; the April 2024 fee rule then eliminated separate biometric fees generally.

SUPERSEDED 2026-03-01. Premium processing fee for I-129 classifications E-1/E-2/E-3/H-1B/H-3/L-1A/L-1B/LZ/O-1/O-2/P/Q-1/TN, in effect 2024-02-26 through 2026-02-28. The I-129 H-2B/R-1 premium fee in this period was $1,685.

Current I-907 premium processing fee for Form I-129 requesting E-1, E-2, E-3, H-1B, H-3, L-1A, L-1B, LZ, O-1, O-2, P-1, P-1S, P-2, P-2S, P-3, P-3S, Q-1, TN-1, or TN-2. Requests postmarked on/after 2026-03-01 must include the new fee. The I-129 H-2B/R-1 premium fee is $1,780 as of the same date. CPI-U-based biennial adjustment (5.72 percent, June 2023 to June 2025); confirmed from the Federal Register final rule text.
SUPERSEDED 2026-03-01. Premium processing fee for Form I-140 (all eligible EB classifications) in effect 2024-02-26 through 2026-02-28.

Current I-907 premium fee for Form I-140, covering E11, E12, E21 (non-NIW), E31, E32, EW3, and E13/E21 NIW classifications. Same $2,965 for NIW despite some secondary sources incorrectly reporting a lower NIW amount - the Federal Register rule text applies $2,805-to-$2,965 to all I-140 premium categories.
SUPERSEDED 2026-03-01. Premium processing fee for Form I-539 (F-1/F-2/J-1/J-2/M-1/M-2 change/extension requests) in effect 2024-02-26 through 2026-02-28.

Current I-907 premium fee for Form I-539 applicants requesting F-1, F-2, J-1, J-2, M-1, or M-2 status. Note: I-539 requests for H-4/L-2 and most other categories are NOT premium-processing eligible as of 2026-07-06.
SUPERSEDED 2026-03-01. Premium processing fee for Form I-765 OPT/STEM OPT categories in effect 2024-02-26 through 2026-02-28.
Current I-907 premium fee for Form I-765 filed by F-1 students in eligible categories: (c)(3)(A) pre-completion OPT, (c)(3)(B) post-completion OPT, (c)(3)(C) STEM OPT extension.

USCIS adjudicative action clock for premium processing of I-129 (all eligible classifications, including H-1B) and I-140 E11/E12/E21(non-NIW)/E31/E32/EW3: 15 business days from receipt of a properly filed I-907. Clock resets after an RFE response.
Premium processing timeframe for I-140 E13 (multinational executive/manager) and E21 NIW classifications: 45 business days. Availability phased in during 2022-2023.

Premium processing timeframe for eligible Form I-765 (F-1 OPT/STEM OPT) and Form I-539 (F/M/J) requests: 30 business days. I-765 OPT premium availability began March 6, 2023; I-539 F/M/J availability phased in June 2023.

rules.source_citation: 22 CFR 22.1; DOS Fees for Visa Services (current as of 2026-07-06)
rules.notes: Nonimmigrant visa application (MRV) fee for non-petition-based visas: B, C-1, D, F (students), I, J, M, TN/TD, S, T, U. Paid with DS-160; nonrefundable; valid 365 days for scheduling. Confirmed on travel.state.gov as of 2026-07-06.

rules.source_citation: 22 CFR 22.1; DOS Fees for Visa Services (current as of 2026-07-06)
rules.notes: MRV fee for petition-based visa categories H (including H-1B and H-4), L, O, P, Q, R. K visas are $265; E treaty visas are $315 (separate row).

rules.source_citation: 22 CFR 22.1; DOS Fees for Visa Services (current as of 2026-07-06)
rules.notes: MRV fee for E-1 treaty trader, E-2 treaty investor, and E-3 Australian specialty occupation visa applications.

rules.value: Varies by nationality and visa class. Charged only if the visa is issued, in addition to the MRV fee, based on the DOS reciprocity schedule for the applicant's country (many H/L applicants, e.g. India, owe $0; others owe class- and duration-specific amounts). Look up per country at the DOS Visa Reciprocity Tables before quoting total consular cost for H-1B/H-4/L-1 issuance.
rules.source_citation: INA sec. 281; DOS Visa Reciprocity Tables
rules.notes: Country-specific amounts were not enumerated in this seed; the platform should resolve them per beneficiary nationality at case time.

NOT YET COLLECTED as of 2026-07-06. HR-1 mandates a minimum $250 Visa Integrity Fee on issuance of most nonimmigrant visas (including H-1B, H-4, L, F-1), CPI-adjustable annually, potentially reimbursable after visa expiry if the holder complied with all conditions. The July 22, 2025 Federal Register notice said implementation requires cross-agency coordination to be addressed in a future publication. INDEPENDENTLY RE-VERIFIED 2026-07-06: the DOS Fees for Visa Services page still does not list any Visa Integrity Fee, and reporting through spring 2026 (Alliance for International Exchange; immigration-bar and news coverage) confirms the fee is still not operational, with no announced start date. Exempt: Visa Waiver Program travelers and immigrant visas. Dependents (e.g., H-4) are NOT exempt once collection begins. Track for an implementation notice.

rules.source_citation: 8 U.S.C. 1807; new 8 CFR 103.7(d)(4); DHS IFR, RIN 1615-AD09 (pub. 2026-04-29, effective 30 days after publication)
rules.notes: HR-1 Form I-94 fee as implemented at USCIS: $24, applicable to Form I-102 (Application for Replacement/Initial Nonimmigrant Arrival-Departure Document), reflected in G-1055 edition 05/29/26. Not waivable; paid separately from the filing fee. Per the April 2026 IFR, at USCIS this fee attaches to I-102 only - it is NOT added to I-129 or I-539 filings. CBP collects the I-94 fee separately for land-border I-94 issuance. FY2026 inflation notice left the $24 unchanged.

rules.source_citation: 20 CFR 655 Subparts H-I; DOL FLAG LCA program page
rules.notes: DOL charges no government fee to file a Labor Condition Application (Form ETA-9035/9035E) for H-1B/H-1B1/E-3. Longstanding; no change found through 2026-07-06.

rules.source_citation: 20 CFR 656; DOL FLAG PERM program page
rules.notes: DOL charges no government fee to file a PERM labor certification (Form ETA-9089). Employer must still bear recruitment costs and, by regulation, PERM costs cannot be shifted to the employee. No change found through 2026-07-06.
